title: oxalis squarrosa
intro: Oxalis squarrosa, also known as the hairy wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant belonging to the Oxalidaceae family, characterized by its hairy stems, leaves, and white or pinkish flowers. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of oxalic acid and other compounds. Consuming Oxalis squarrosa has been reported to have health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatic pain, and aiding in digestion. Oxalis squarrosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice, making it a versatile herbal remedy that can be used in various forms to support overall well-being.
